qviewparsR 1.0.0

Reading Q-View project files with qviewparsR

Source: vignettes/qviewparsR.Rmd
qviewparsR.Rmd

qviewparsR is a pure-R parser for .Q-View binary project files. The file format is a single-file container that bundles an embedded H2 SQL database with binary plate-image data; the rendered CSV report is stored as a CLOB inside the database. read_qview() extracts that report without needing a Java runtime or H2 driver.

Reading a project file 

qv <- read_qview("path/to/project.Q-View")
qv

The returned object has class qview and contains:

str(qv, max.level = 1)
#> List of 11
#>  $ metadata          :List of 12
#>  $ manifest          : tibble [4 x 3]
#>  $ segments          : tibble [3 x 4]
#>  $ analytes          : tibble [<n_analytes> x 8]
#>  $ well_groups       : tibble [<n_groups> x 7]
#>  $ pixel_intensities : tibble [<n_rows> x 8]
#>  $ summary_statistics: tibble [<n_rows> x 6]
#>  $ concentrations    : NULL or tibble
#>  $ curve_fit         : NULL or tibble
#>  $ report_csv        : character vector
#>  $ plate_layout      : tibble [96 x 7]
#>  - attr(*, "class") = "qview"

The naming convention

When Q-View imports a plate-template CSV it rewrites the identifiers:

  • Cal 1 -> ICal 1
  • Low -> GLow
  • High -> HHigh
  • FD... and any all-digit ID -> a single N prefix.

Pass strip_prefix = TRUE to undo the rewrite:

qv <- read_qview("path/to/project.Q-View", strip_prefix = TRUE)
qv$well_groups$sample_id

Plotting 

plot(qv, type = "plate_map")
plot(qv, type = "intensity_heatmap")
plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")

plot.qview() requires ggplot2.

Exporting 

qview_to_xlsx(qv, "out.xlsx")
qview_to_csv_dir(qv, "out_csv/")
saveRDS(qv, "out.rds")

Interactive front-end 

qview_app()

Launches a Shiny app: upload a .Q-View file (and optionally a plate-template CSV), preview each parsed table, visualise the plate map, intensity heatmap, and replicate scatter, and download the result as xlsx, rds, or a zip of CSVs.

On this page

Developed by Raban Heller.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
